column1: # OWASP ZAP passive/active scan rules — DeelMarkt staging
# Actions: IGNORE (suppress), WARN (report but no fail), FAIL (hard fail)
# Flutter web and Cloudflare edge introduce patterns that trigger ZAP
# false positives. Suppress known-safe findings here; review annually.
# Content Security Policy — Flutter web bootstrap requires 'unsafe-inline'
# for its inline script tag. This is a framework constraint, not a project flaw.

column3: Flutter web requires CSP unsafe-inline in bootstrap; not exploitable

column1: # X-Frame-Options — set at the Cloudflare edge (not in app-level headers).
# ZAP scans the origin, not the CDN layer, so it doesn't see the edge header.
# WARN (not IGNORE) so we detect if edge config drifts and stops emitting the header.

column3: X-Frame-Options set at Cloudflare edge; WARN to catch edge config drift

column1: # Storable and Cacheable Content — CDN caching is intentional for static assets.

column3: CDN cache headers on static Flutter web assets are by design

column1: # Server header leaks — Supabase and Cloudflare add server headers that
# cannot be stripped without paid-tier WAF rules.

column3: Server header exposed by upstream (Supabase/Cloudflare); low risk

column1: # Missing Anti-CSRF — Flutter web SPA uses Authorization headers (not cookies)
# for all state-mutating requests; CSRF via form POST is not applicable for SPA endpoints.
# WARN (not IGNORE) to surface any cookie-authenticated admin/Supabase endpoints if they appear.

column3: SPA uses Bearer tokens; WARN to catch any future cookie-auth endpoints

column1: # Timestamp disclosure — Supabase response timestamps are intentional metadata.

column3: Supabase timestamps in JSON responses are intentional API data
